pattern_recognition: Tree Comparison - This problem falls under the category of tree traversal and comparison. The signals include the need to check both structure and values of two binary trees.

approaches.title: Brute Force
approaches.intuition: Compare each node of both trees recursively to check for equality.
approaches.algorithm: 1. If both nodes are null, return true. 2. If one is null and the other is not, return false. 3. If values of both nodes are different, return false. 4. Recursively check left and right subtrees.

approaches.title: Optimal Approach
approaches.intuition: Use a single traversal method to compare both trees simultaneously, reducing the need for multiple checks.
approaches.algorithm: 1. Use a queue to perform level-order traversal. 2. Compare nodes from both trees at each level. 3. If any mismatch is found, return false.

dry_run: Consider two trees: tree1 = [1, 2, 3] and tree2 = [1, 2, 3]. Start at the root: both are 1, continue to left children (2 vs 2) and right children (3 vs 3). All nodes match, so return true.
edge_cases: Both trees are empty, one tree is empty while the other is not, trees with only one node, trees with duplicate values, large trees with many nodes, and trees that cause integer overflow.
common_pitfalls: 1. Failing to check both trees for null nodes properly can lead to incorrect results. 2. Not considering the structure of the trees can lead to false positives. 3. Overlooking the need for simultaneous traversal can result in inefficiencies.
